question.label: Team connection
question.name: How connected do you feel to the rest of the team right now?
question.description: Probes isolation or drift without naming it; one of the earliest signals of disengagement.

answer: I have been less present in rituals, yes.
note: He directly confirms reduced participation in team rituals, which is a strong engagement dip and a mild wellbeing concern.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: have been less — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.
note: He links being less present to unclear expectations after the promotion decision, a strong engagement drop; also reveals a clarity issue worth probing next.

question.label: Promotion feedback
question.name: What feedback from the promotion decision actually stuck with you?
question.description: Surfaces what he heard versus what was intended, and whether it became usable.

answer: I stayed focused on delivery because that felt controllable.
note: He defaulted to delivery because it felt controllable, which signals the promotion feedback did not translate into clear guidance; also hints at a narrowed growth posture.

question.label: Controllable delivery
question.name: When delivery felt controllable, what felt unclear or out of reach?
question.description: Drills into his 'delivery felt controllable' comment to surface what the promotion feedback left vague.

answer: Mentoring has been one place where I still feel useful.
note: He answered a clarity probe by locating usefulness in mentoring instead of naming what became clear, which signals the path still feels vague; also surfaced mentoring as a concrete growth thread.

question.label: Mentoring usefulness
question.name: Mentoring feels useful — what is it giving you that other parts of the role aren't?
question.description: Drills into mentoring as the concrete thread to surface what need it is meeting right now.

answer: I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.
note: He explicitly doubts whether recognized work matches what he is doing, a strong clarity misalignment signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'the work that gets recognized' merits follow-up next session.

question.label: Energy read
question.name: How's your energy been lately — separately from how work is going?
question.description: Separates energy from output; avoids leading with 'is something wrong'.

answer: I want clearer criteria, not reassurance.
note: Pivot/off-topic from an energy question into a request for clearer criteria; no wellbeing signal scored, but strong clarity need is evident and worth taking up next.

question.label: Clearer criteria
question.name: When you say 'clearer criteria,' what specifically feels missing?
question.description: Follows his stated need without diagnosing emotion; surfaces the concrete clarity gap underneath the frustration.

answer: I can own bigger direction, but I need real authority to do it.
note: He names a concrete clarity deficit: he believes he can own bigger direction but lacks the authority to do it. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'real authority' could be worth unpacking next session.

question.label: Support needed
question.name: What support from me would make the next couple of weeks steadier for you?
question.description: Closes by identifying concrete support without opening a new topic.

answer: If we can define the path precisely, I will commit to it.
note: He names precise path definition as the needed support, which signals a mild clarity gap alongside willingness to commit once expectations are clear.

question.label: Priority ranking
question.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?
question.description: Forces a real priority ranking. Vague answers are a clarity signal.

answer: I would start by naming one forum where I want clearer criteria, and what support I need to show up there.
note: He identifies a concrete next step around a forum needing clearer criteria, which shows some initiative but ongoing lack of role/expectation clarity.
